source_texts: “What about that!” Lizaveta Prokofyevna thought to herself. “Most of the time she just eats and sleeps and shows no sign of life, and then about once a year she’ll get up and say something that’ll leave everyone speechless.”
‘When I wished to determine the increase in weight the Nautilus would require to dive, my only concern was the progressive reduction in volume that sea water experiences as it becomes deeper.’

This was the philosophy worked out by the Oblomovka Plato, a philosophy which lulled him amid the issues and strict demands of duty and purpose! He had been born and reared not like a gladiator, for the arena, but as a peaceful spectator of the battle. It was not for his meek and lazy soul to endure either the alarms of happiness or life’s blows. Consequently, his person expressed one of life’s extremes, and he did nothing to achieve, change, or repent.
“Wait, you didn’t get that from me!” Ivan suddenly stopped, struck by this fact. “It never even occurred to me . . . Strange!”

(and Apollon Apollonovich could not form a clear and distinct idea of into what exactly). At any rate, Apollon Apollonovich would lose his new position: he could not accept it until he had washed away the disgraceful spots which had been made by his unworthy son and which had besmirched his honor.
yells Mikolka, the crowbar still in his hands, his eyes bloodshot. He stands there as if feeling sorry that there’s nothing more to strike.
Nikolai Apollonovich perceived a shady type (perhaps of the petty bourgeoisie) with his neck wrapped up.

Once the insurgents were driven back from the top of the barricade by cannon-ball and from the windows of the tavern by canister-shot, the attacking columns would be able to venture into the street without being picked off, perhaps even without being seen, quickly scale the redoubt as on the preceding evening and – who knows? – take it by surprise.
“Sit down, Ivan, please, here on the sofa,” Alyosha said, frightened. “You’re feverish. Lie down. Put your head on the pillow. Right. Would you like a wet towel on your head? It might help.”

How could I possibly have known that he’d come here with his finger half bitten off? Otherwise, really I might have hidden them, just as you say. I see that you have at last come up with a clever remark today.”
rationales: Outputs 0 and 1 both use third-person narrative framing rather than direct quoted thought, both employ 'completely' as a modifier (lifeless/inactive), describe the woman as doing 'little more than eating and sleeping,' and conclude with similar phrasing about listeners being stunned or at a loss. Their formal, distanced narrative register and structural parallelism strongly suggest they came from the same paraphraser.

Outputs 2 and 4 share nearly identical phrasing in their key clause: 'gradual decrease in the volume of seawater as depth increases,' which is a strong stylistic fingerprint. Both also use 'submerge' and frame the weight in similar terms ('additional weight' vs 'extra weight'). This level of lexical overlap in the core technical phrase strongly suggests they come from the same paraphraser.

Outputs 0 and 2 share distinctive linguistic fingerprints: both use 'gentle and idle spirit' (a unique phrase not found in others), both describe the protagonist as a 'calm observer,' both use 'fighter' instead of 'gladiator,' both use 'soothed him' with similar framing, both describe 'one end/extreme of human existence,' and both use 'hardships' in parallel contexts. Their sentence structures and word choices are consistently similar, suggesting they came from the same paraphrasing system.

Outputs 2 and 4 share nearly identical phrasing throughout: both open with 'Hold on—you didn't hear that from me!' (with the em-dash), both use 'Ivan broke off abruptly' followed by a brief description of realization, and both close with 'It never even crossed my mind... How odd!' The only differences are minor: 'as the realization hit him' vs. 'realizing it,' and 'never even' vs. 'hadn't even.' These small variations are consistent with the same paraphraser producing two slightly different outputs.

Outputs 0 and 3 share nearly identical structure, vocabulary, and phrasing throughout: both use 'forfeit his new post,' 'cleansed the shameful stains,' 'unworthy son,' and 'tarnished his honor.' The only differences are minor substitutions ('imagine what it actually was' vs 'understand what, exactly'), which is characteristic of the same paraphraser making small word-level variations while preserving the overall template.

Outputs 2 and 4 share nearly identical phrasing: both use 'red and wild' to describe eyes, both use 'stands motionless, as though disappointed that there is nothing left to hit' (word-for-word identical), and both follow the same sentence structure throughout. The only minor difference is 'clutching the crowbar in his hands' vs 'still gripping the crowbar,' which is a trivial variation likely from the same paraphraser.

Outputs 2 and 3 share the same structure and phrasing most closely: both use 'possibly,' 'lower middle class' (unhyphenated in 3, hyphenated in 2), and describe the neck wrapping with parallel construction ('something wrapped around his neck' vs. 'whose neck was wrapped'). Output 1 differs by using 'petty bourgeois' and 'perhaps,' and Output 0 uses parentheses and 'covered up,' making them distinct. Outputs 2 and 3 are the most linguistically similar pair.

Outputs 2 and 4 share the same archaic/literary style and nearly identical sentence structure: both begin with 'The combatants once driven from the... barricade by balls... and from the windows of the [wine-shop/cabaret] by grape[-shot],' both use 'attacking columns could venture into the street,' 'scale the redoubt,' and retain the rhetorical 'who knows?' phrase. The syntactic skeleton and word choices are far more similar to each other than to any other output, indicating they came from the same paraphrasing system.

Outputs 2 and 3 share highly distinctive linguistic fingerprints: both use 'damp' (cloth/towel) instead of 'wet towel,' both include the interjection 'That's it,' both use the phrasing 'do you good/some good,' and both employ direct speech with similar sentence structures and word choices. These specific details (especially 'damp' and 'That's it') are unique to these two outputs and strongly suggest the same paraphraser.

Outputs 0 and 3 share the same declarative opening structure ('I had no way of knowing he would show up with...'), both use 'just as you suggested,' and both employ a similarly formal, straightforward paraphrasing style. Their sentence constructions and word choices are closely aligned compared to the rhetorical-question style of outputs 2 and 4.
